branches.condition: Developer chooses to verify a sending domain instead of a single sender email
branches.effect_on_first_success: Allows sending from any email address on the domain with DKIM and SPF benefits

branches.condition: Developer uses SMTP integration instead of API or SDK
branches.effect_on_first_success: Enables sending email by switching SMTP credentials without code changes

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: API response success with message_id and acceptance confirmation

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer sends a test transactional email that is accepted by Postmark and visible in the Postmark UI or confirmed by API response
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: API response indicates success with a message ID; email appears in Postmark activity or test inbox
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send an email through Postmark using the API or SDK
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official docs show that receiving a successful API response with a message ID confirms the email was accepted for delivery, marking the first successful use of the platform.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has an email address to use as a sender signature
Developer has access to a domain for sending email (optional for initial testing)
entry_point.developer_intent: Send transactional email using Postmark's API or SDKs to verify successful email delivery.

friction_gates.description: Creating and verifying a Postmark account requires email confirmation.
Sender signature email address must be confirmed via a confirmation email before use.
Domain verification requires adding DKIM and SPF DNS records to the domain's DNS provider.
API requests require a valid Server API token in the HTTP header for authentication.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Postmark account by signing up at postmarkapp.com and verifying the account email.
Create a Postmark server (sub-account) within the account to organize sending environments.
Obtain a Server API token from the Postmark server's API Tokens tab for authentication.
Add and confirm a sender signature email address to use as the 'From' address in emails.

primary_path.action: Sign up for a Postmark account and verify email
details: Check email inbox for verification link and confirm
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Verification email not received or link expired
primary_path.input: User registration details and email confirmation

primary_path.action: Create a Postmark server to organize sending
details: Create a new server in the Postmark dashboard
Optionally create sandbox server for testing
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Server creation errors or UI issues
primary_path.output: Postmark server with unique API tokens
primary_path.success_signal: Server appears in dashboard with API tokens

primary_path.action: Retrieve Server API token from server settings
details: Copy Server Token for use in API calls
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Token not visible or permissions issues
primary_path.success_signal: Token copied and ready for use

primary_path.action: Add and confirm a sender signature email address
details: Add sender signature or domain in dashboard
Confirm ownership via email confirmation link
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Confirmation email not received or expired
primary_path.input: Email address to use as sender

primary_path.action: Install official Postmark SDK or prepare API request
details: Choose official SDK for preferred language or prepare HTTP request
Install SDK via package manager if applicable
primary_path.input: SDK installation command or API request setup
primary_path.output: SDK ready or API request prepared
primary_path.success_signal: SDK installed or API request ready

primary_path.action: Send a test email using the SDK or API with the Server Token and confirmed sender signature
details: Use example code from official docs
Send email to a test recipient
primary_path.failure_or_wait: API errors such as 401 Unauthorized or 400 Bad Request
primary_path.input: Email parameters (from, to, subject, body) and Server Token
primary_path.output: API response with success and message ID

sections_used: Overview of transactional vs broadcast email
sources.title: Postmark Manual
Getting Started With Postmark In 5 Steps - YouTube
Postmark: Fast, Reliable Email Delivery Service

alternatives_considered.name: SMTP integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: While SMTP is supported and enabled by default for quick setup, the official docs recommend using the REST API and official SDKs for deeper integration and advanced features.

surface.name: REST API and official SDKs
surface.selection_basis: Postmark's official developer documentation emphasizes the REST API and provides multiple official SDKs for integration, including Python, Ruby, .NET, PHP, Java, and Node.js, as well as SMTP integration.
